[2018-07-18 04:51] supergreyman (Greyman): **Anti-Thermal Cannon** 3 :Gold: :Blue: :Red: (10 supply) 4 :Health: 
:Frontline:
Click: Pay :Energy::Energy: to :Chill: 5
[2018-07-18 04:53] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): so you line it up with the lifespan on dgrid/tank to get the guaranteed freeze before it can get killed?
[2018-07-18 04:55] .jrkirby (jrkirby): Interesting, you gotta spend a lot of defensive tempo to turn it on
[2018-07-18 04:56] .jrkirby (jrkirby): really high value if you can get the tempo though
[2018-07-18 04:56] .jrkirby (jrkirby): 3GBR for 4 soak is pretty great, and that's only after it gets the potential 7 freeze value
[2018-07-18 04:57] supergreyman (Greyman): Gotta make sure it competes with Vai. 😄
[2018-07-18 04:58] supergreyman (Greyman): I think the Lifespan is somewhat unnecessary.
[2018-07-18 04:58] supergreyman (Greyman): Maybe I should take it off.
[2018-07-18 05:00] kaelii: I donno how big the absorber has to be to want to actually run one of these out. Certainly on Dgrid its nice but would you buy them against wall absorb?
[2018-07-18 05:00] supergreyman (Greyman): Yeah, the idea is that it's really great against huge blockers, but it comes at a cost of high energy.
[2018-07-18 05:00] supergreyman (Greyman): It's certainly not great in BSO.
[2018-07-18 05:01] supergreyman (Greyman): I think of it as Tatsu but for pure chill. But maybe this is overly specialized.
[2018-07-18 05:01] .jrkirby (jrkirby): Yeah 5 and higher absorb definitely you want this. Maybe even 5 HP stuff too?
[2018-07-18 05:01] .jrkirby (jrkirby): also good if they can get out plxos
[2018-07-18 05:02] kaelii: Also depends on if there are other decent freeze alternatives out there.
[2018-07-18 05:02] kaelii: If you have nivos how often do you just want to nivo instead of ATC?
[2018-07-18 05:02] .jrkirby (jrkirby): should be fine against botches timing out in high ecn games
[2018-07-18 05:04] supergreyman (Greyman): Yeah, the HP could be bumped a little more.
[2018-07-18 05:04] kaelii: The most painful part of buying it is easily the blue cost, as usually blue wants to be going into wall or better soak in the random units.
[2018-07-18 05:06] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): I guess you need the blue to justify the high hp
[2018-07-18 05:06] supergreyman (Greyman): Not really much of a problem.
[2018-07-18 05:06] supergreyman (Greyman): It's dirt cheap for massive chill.
[2018-07-18 05:07] supergreyman (Greyman): (except the dirt cheap part)
[2018-07-18 05:10] .jrkirby (jrkirby): If I had a hand on it, I'd make it a bit less extreme in all ways
[2018-07-18 05:11] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): Sick Draw (1 :Gold: :Blue: , 1 Supply) 8 :Health: 
99 :Lifespan:
[2018-07-18 05:11] .jrkirby (jrkirby): 3 HP, 5 freeze, 2 energy needed, no lifespan
[2018-07-18 05:13] .jrkirby (jrkirby): The way it is, it's extremely hard to get it to 'work', but if you did get it to 'work' it's almost OP
[2018-07-18 05:13] .jrkirby (jrkirby): And that's fair, but it feels a bit extreme
[2018-07-18 05:13] supergreyman (Greyman): Yeah, it needs a bit more middle ground.
[2018-07-18 05:14] supergreyman (Greyman): A bit too specialized as of right now.
[2018-07-18 05:19] supergreyman (Greyman): The more I look at it, the more I feel the energy click cost is completely unnecessary.

[2018-07-18 05:59] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): Weaver (7:Gold: :Energy: :Energy: :Energy: :Engineer:, Supply 4) :Fragile: 1

When bought consume an :Engineer:

Start of each player's turn: Current player sacrifices a unit with 1 :Health:/:Fragile:

(Passive/Static abilities don't exist in game right now, effects for this unit should be immediate so that the opponent is affected first, but I'll have to leave it like this).
[2018-07-18 06:00] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): wait it can sac itself
[2018-07-18 06:00] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): I'll post the same thing i posted on the thread
eww, adding more phases
[2018-07-18 06:00] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): why is it breaking sdr? for some reason all my units do. I need to get my head out of MTG, maybe?
[2018-07-18 06:01] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): since it can sac itself, it doesn't break afaik
[2018-07-18 06:01] namington: its a bit weird though
[2018-07-18 06:01] namington: like, say both players have one
[2018-07-18 06:01] namington: which goes first? do they go pseudo-simultaneously?
[2018-07-18 06:01] namington: if both players have one, and you sack your weaver, do you get another weaver sack?
[2018-07-18 06:02] namington: but yeah it doesnt break SDR cause, if you ever dont want it, you can sac it
[2018-07-18 06:02] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): I guess you don't need a stack in this game, so you can do it simultaneously
[2018-07-18 06:02] namington: funnily enough, itd break SDR if it was buffed to 2 health
[2018-07-18 06:03] namington: @jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer) I guess that works, probably would feel a bit weird - does it insert its "phase" before or after the blocking phase?
[2018-07-18 06:03] namington: it'd probably be better gameplay-wise to put it before, except then that interrupts the seamless flow of the game
[2018-07-18 06:03] namington: and is probably unintuitive to newer players especially
[2018-07-18 06:03] namington: "he just ended his turn, why aren't I getting attacked?"
[2018-07-18 06:05] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): you bring up an interesting point. I think it should be after blockers, but then that's gonna mess with the undo.
[2018-07-18 06:06] namington: idk, its an interesting mechanic but it'd be really unintuitive in practice I feel
[2018-07-18 06:06] namington: I also dont think it ever gets bought
[2018-07-18 06:06] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): crap, that makes it overly complicated. but would be nice. anyways, that's my baby
[2018-07-18 06:07] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): it's like back tracking on going high econ should. be interesting
[2018-07-18 06:07] f300xen (f0x): "your opponent can't buy lucina"
[2018-07-18 06:07] namington: like, I guess itd be neat if your opponent ONLY had drones/tarsiers, but its still not really worth 7 + an engi in that case
[2018-07-18 06:07] namington: @f0x they can buy lucina, they just cant run out of drones
[2018-07-18 06:08] f300xen (f0x): didn't you know that the main application of lucina is breachproof smh
[2018-07-18 06:08] namington: its basically a cluster bolt
[2018-07-18 06:08] namington: except it costs red instead
[2018-07-18 06:08] namington: and yknow, 17 more gold, but forget that part
[2018-07-18 06:09] f300xen (f0x): sometimes breachproof games go to supply
[2018-07-18 06:10] kaelii: Would you rather pay 16 (over multiple turns) for 4 attack per turn but your opponent gets 1 attack per turn, and the option to get other things instead of attack, OR pay 17RRRR for 4 attack per turn and you can spend red to turn a drone into 2 HP threat soak next turn?

[2018-07-18 17:30] supergreyman (Greyman): **Gauss Giant** 8 :Gold: :Green: :Green: :Green: (4 supply)
5 :Fragile:, :Blocker:
Click: Pay :Energy::Energy: to gain :Attack: :Attack: and restore 1 :Fragile: (up to a maximum of 12 :Fragile:)
[2018-07-18 17:32] supergreyman (Greyman): I'm just making up number here. 😄
[2018-07-18 17:33] supergreyman (Greyman): Maybe 12 is a bit better.
[2018-07-18 17:33] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): It grows as it attacks, that's interesting. But seems undercosted (just my intuition, since I know very little about design)
[2018-07-18 17:33] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): By undercosted I mostly mean the click ability.
[2018-07-18 17:35] supergreyman (Greyman): Yeah, it's a bit on the strong side.
[2018-07-18 17:35] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): Funny that you can't go breachproof with it. Intentional?
[2018-07-18 17:37] supergreyman (Greyman): Yeah, it's meant to be that way. The idea is to keep attacking and see when the inflation is high enough to be worth sacrificing.
[2018-07-18 17:39] supergreyman (Greyman): I think bumping the cost to 8 :Gold: should be enough.
[2018-07-18 17:39] supergreyman (Greyman): It is a little hard to buy due to triple green cost.
[2018-07-18 17:43] darkeisbein: 12 cost for 2 attack, :monkaS:
[2018-07-18 17:44] darkeisbein: and a great hold on top
[2018-07-18 17:44] darkeisbein: like this is just super budget colossus that grows back
[2018-07-18 17:45] supergreyman (Greyman): Ha, that's why I need you. 😄
[2018-07-18 17:45] supergreyman (Greyman): Didn't think of it that way.
[2018-07-18 17:47] supergreyman (Greyman): Yeah, this thing is way too undercosted.
[2018-07-18 17:48] supergreyman (Greyman): I was thinking in terms of Electrovore + Gauss Cannon
[2018-07-18 17:48] supergreyman (Greyman): But the ability to block and grow back HP should make it a lot higher.
[2018-07-18 17:50] supergreyman (Greyman): The energy click cost seems somewhat silly too.
[2018-07-18 17:50] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): Using it for 5 hp and the a bt2 double attacker is pretty valuable.
[2018-07-18 17:51] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): One thing I was wondering about for the click cost was if you could click it when it was already at Max hp
[2018-07-18 17:55] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): I could headcannon the energy cost to be causing the gaussite to grow
[2018-07-18 17:57] supergreyman (Greyman): Yeah, I'm thinking of increasing the click cost to :Energy::Energy: since it would make it a bit harder to click continuously.
[2018-07-18 17:58] supergreyman (Greyman): So it would become like buying 2 Electrovores but gaining super beefy HP at the end.
[2018-07-18 17:59] supergreyman (Greyman): Depending on how long you can keep getting engineers.

2 Buildtime :Time:
Click: pay :Energy: to gain 4 :Gold: . Exhaust 3 :Exhaust:
[2018-07-19 02:42] swapgo: You're gonna have to run me through that name
[2018-07-19 02:43] swapgo: Also this seems like a huge tempo loss?
[2018-07-19 02:43] swapgo: 2 Energy means no drones, Buildtime means no upside
[2018-07-19 02:43] swapgo: it might help some drone cutting openings like P2 Fastimus and P1 Blastduit
[2018-07-19 02:43] .jrkirby (jrkirby): It's a drone with a swingy, offbalance tempo. It's got quite good value, but it's awkward to build.
[2018-07-19 02:43] swapgo: Also I think you mean Spend E to gain 4G
[2018-07-19 02:45] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): " :Click: , and pay :Energy: : gain 4 :Gold: and :Exhaust: 3 "
[2018-07-19 02:47] swapgo: Let me just think this in openings for a second
[2018-07-19 02:48] swapgo: You're P2 and you go DD, 1A, TT, then on turn 4 you have 14 Gold
[2018-07-19 02:49] swapgo: Your production in a few turns is crap though, but you can squeeze in a B or C and still grow attack
[2018-07-19 02:50] swapgo: Sounds like it'd be playable in sets with tech-intensive, but cheap units (Like Hellhound, Feral Warden, Fibroid, etc)
[2018-07-19 02:50] swapgo: The :Energy: cost on click feels necessary, else you'd easily recoup on drones on the swing turn  without having to invest on engies
[2018-07-19 02:51] .jrkirby (jrkirby): I think it's generally pretty good as the last couple drones you get
[2018-07-19 02:51] .jrkirby (jrkirby): Ignoring energy costs, it has much higher value than drones
[2018-07-19 02:51] swapgo: That is definitely true
[2018-07-19 02:51] .jrkirby (jrkirby): but it's much more complicated to play
[2018-07-19 02:52] swapgo: I dig the design, there might be issues with it, it feels like very difficult to evaluate, but very powerful if timed right
[2018-07-19 02:58] .jrkirby (jrkirby): I specifically made it exhaust 3 and supply 4 so that if you buy them all, there becomes a sort of rhythm to your economy. It's also buildtime 2 and exhaust 3 so that even if you want exactly 3 for a smooth flowing economy, you have to carefully plan the third so you'll have 3 engineers to click the first and buy the third.
[2018-07-19 02:59] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): 33.3% RoI assuming E=.3 for the entire game?
[2018-07-19 03:02] .jrkirby (jrkirby): It's pretty close to that, yeah, a little better.
[2018-07-19 03:03] .jrkirby (jrkirby): maybe like 36% RoI for E=1/3
[2018-07-19 03:05] .jrkirby (jrkirby): Not quite wild drone that never dies level of value, but pretty close
[2018-07-19 03:07] .jrkirby (jrkirby): actually, if energy is considered free, it's like within a couple percent of wild drone that never dies value

[2018-07-19 16:19] supergreyman (Greyman): Ok, got some possibly bad unit for you:

**Shock Bomb** 1 :Gold: :Energy: :Energy: (20 supply)
1 :Health:
Click: Sacrifice Shock Bomb to gain :Attack: and construct an Engineer for your opponent.
[2018-07-19 16:20] shadourow (Rando): Soooo.... that's a 4E pixie ? 😐
[2018-07-19 16:22] kristian1103 (Arkanishu): if it blocks its an engineer with threat
[2018-07-19 16:22] supergreyman (Greyman): Well, it is tech-less pixie, so it might be easy to spam.
[2018-07-19 16:22] shadourow (Rando): I guess that with blocker, it's ok
[2018-07-19 16:22] supergreyman (Greyman): Not sure if it's OP with 1 :Gold:
[2018-07-19 16:22] shadourow (Rando): But without, it need to cost max 1E imo, or maybe that's broken with antima ?
[2018-07-19 16:23] kristian1103 (Arkanishu): if it doesn't block it's still pretty garbage at 1
[2018-07-19 16:23] kristian1103 (Arkanishu): if you kill an engineer you pay 3 gold to kill 2 gold
[2018-07-19 16:24] kristian1103 (Arkanishu): and lose the opportunity to buy better units with that gold
[2018-07-19 16:24] supergreyman (Greyman): I was comparing this to a Pixie that cost about 2.66 gold.
[2018-07-19 16:24] olafim (olaf_im): Maybe it'd work with exhaust 2 or 3 an engineer
[2018-07-19 16:24] shadourow (Rando): Pixie is nice when B is free imo
[2018-07-19 16:25] kristian1103 (Arkanishu): pixie is garbage unit as well, you'd never buy it if you have any other way to not waste ressources
[2018-07-19 16:26] supergreyman (Greyman): If I lower the cost to 1 :Gold:, wouldn't it be too easy to create threat?
[2018-07-19 16:26] kristian1103 (Arkanishu): a unit that just costs gold will never have that use.. because you can always spend your gold on engis if nothing else
[2018-07-19 16:26] kristian1103 (Arkanishu): well you have to buy the engineers as well, and the unit sucks so much your opponent won't care much
[2018-07-19 16:27] supergreyman (Greyman): Gotcha, I got another idea. If the click ability doesn't consume an Engineer but instead creates one for your opponent. Would it still be bad?
[2018-07-19 16:28] supergreyman (Greyman): Click: Sacrifice Shock Bomb to gain :Attack: and construct an Engineer for your opponent.
[2018-07-19 16:28] olafim (olaf_im): Maybe a bt 1 barrier is better
[2018-07-19 16:29] supergreyman (Greyman): (That was my original idea actually)
[2018-07-19 16:29] supergreyman (Greyman): Like Valkyrion style?
[2018-07-19 16:30] shadourow (Rando): That second version is interesting indeed
[2018-07-19 16:30] olafim (olaf_im): Then it's good as first attack cause opponent won't be defending next turn
[2018-07-19 16:32] supergreyman (Greyman): I think it's overall more interesting, but I'm always worried if the threat is too strong.
[2018-07-19 16:35] supergreyman (Greyman): I think the barrier idea would be extremely abusable.
[2018-07-19 16:35] olafim (olaf_im): I think not cause buying it early instead of a drone isn't so good and buying it later is a worse pixie
[2018-07-19 16:37] supergreyman (Greyman): Yeah, it's not meant to be bought in early game. During the late game, it would be usable if you can use it to kill something more useful than an Engineer.
[2018-07-19 16:38] supergreyman (Greyman): Using it to threaten breach is pretty good.
[2018-07-19 16:38] rezyk: i think it's comparable to Chill 1
[2018-07-19 16:40] supergreyman (Greyman): Yeah, it's quite comparable.
[2018-07-19 16:41] supergreyman (Greyman): It's not the same, but they're similar in giving pressure for cheap cost.

--- Thread (24 messages, 2018-07-20) ---
[2018-07-20 05:03] supergreyman (Greyman): **Crazy Drone** 3 :Gold: :Energy: (1 supply)
:Blocker:, 1 :Health:
Click: Gain 4 :Gold: and construct a Doomed Drone for your opponent
[2018-07-20 05:06] supergreyman (Greyman): I've done some math, and it seems to be about the same efficiency a regular Drone. Not sure how crazy your first turn opening would be, but oh well.
[2018-07-20 05:06] xyotsuba: Isn’t this hella op?
[2018-07-20 05:07] supergreyman (Greyman): It's pretty strong, but both players have access to it, so I'm sure they'll have a pretty crazy experience.
[2018-07-20 05:07] xyotsuba: I feel like 4 gold now is way better than a doomed drone
[2018-07-20 05:09] xyotsuba: You actually have to be careful when using these tho not to undertech yourself
[2018-07-20 05:09] supergreyman (Greyman): It is great, but is it worth 3 gold?
[2018-07-20 05:10] xyotsuba: Oh it’s only 1 supply
[2018-07-20 05:10] supergreyman (Greyman): Yeah, definitely not a full replacement of regular Drone.
[2018-07-20 05:10] supergreyman (Greyman): Or else we'll have too many Doomed Drone spam.
[2018-07-20 05:11] .bky_1556 (bky): Re: Shock Bomb - it's purely for threat. It gets *very* degenerate in Centurion mirrors.
[2018-07-20 05:11] .bky_1556 (bky): As soon as one player buys a Shock Bomb, the other needs an extra engineer to defend the threat.
[2018-07-20 05:12] .bky_1556 (bky): Then they have extra energy.
[2018-07-20 05:12] .bky_1556 (bky): That means the best use of their gold is more Shock Bombs to threaten the opponent's Centurion.
[2018-07-20 05:12] .bky_1556 (bky): So both sides end up stockpiling the full stack of Shock Bombs and not using them.
[2018-07-20 05:13] xyotsuba: That’s just a badly designed unit then
[2018-07-20 05:13] supergreyman (Greyman): Yeah, I figure it might be too strong.
[2018-07-20 05:14] supergreyman (Greyman): A gold to force a Forcefield is too much.
[2018-07-20 05:15] .bky_1556 (bky): What about 2 gold to force a field?
[2018-07-20 05:16] supergreyman (Greyman): Might be alright, but it's pretty hard to balance something like this.
[2018-07-20 05:16] .bky_1556 (bky): 1EE? That way it's telegraphed.
[2018-07-20 05:16] .bky_1556 (bky): Still has the problem where it's defended with an engineer that's then used to stockpile shock bombs.
[2018-07-20 05:21] supergreyman (Greyman): At least you won't stockpile as quickly.
[2018-07-20 05:22] supergreyman (Greyman): I think the overall design is pretty poor.

[2018-07-21 09:15] namington: I think it's fairly common knowledge that Flame Animus is on the more efficient end of units, and also one of the more econ-warping ones
[2018-07-21 09:15] namington: like, just by existing, flanimus often forces t2-3 blastforge even if the rest of the set doesnt really support it
[2018-07-21 09:15] namington: Is this econ-warping effect, combined with its sheer efficiency, problematic design-wise to the point of deserving a rework?
[2018-07-21 09:16] namington: and if so, how should such a rework be pursued? I know that old flame animus was even more dumb cause whoever got it first either won an engi or forced a wall from the opponent
[2018-07-21 09:16] namington: but I feel like just "changing the numbers" like increasing its cost would make it near-unbuyable in most sets
[2018-07-21 09:17] masn6811 (Masne98888): its strong, but part of the reason its bought so early isnt the efficiency
[2018-07-21 09:17] f300xen (f0x): nerf tarsier, tarsier is too strong as flame animus support in most flanimus sets
[2018-07-21 09:17] masn6811 (Masne98888): it basically turns your forge into a buildtime 3 halfimus, buildtime 4 once you want your second red
[2018-07-21 09:18] masn6811 (Masne98888): and since red is worth so much early and drops off in value late, you kinda have to get it early in order to find the red in time
